title: micromeria filiformis subsp. cordata
intro: Micromeria filiformis subsp. cordata, also known as the Corsican mint or Corsican thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Micromeria filiformis subsp. cordata include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
